title: Thelypteris chrysodioides
intro: Thelypteris chrysodioides, also known as the golden sori fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae, characterized by its delicate fronds and distinctive golden spores. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Thelypteris chrysodioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Thelypteris chrysodioides is often prepared as a tea or infusion, or used as a component in topical creams and ointments to support skin health and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Thelypteris chrysodioides belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. This species is a type of fern, and more specifically, it is classified within the genus Thelypteris. The classification of this species is further detailed within the realm of botany and plant taxonomy.
